[[Category:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]]{{Shrine |name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kawaguchi-asama-jinja haiden and sugi (1).JPG|270px]]
"Gonishirushi" of the Seven Sacred Cedars and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|31|51.8|N|138|46|29.8|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District|ja|富士河口湖町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}} |Latitude degrees = 35 |Latitude minutes = 31 |Latitude seconds = 51.84 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 46 |Longitude seconds = 29.87 |ISO = JP-19 |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) {{ill|Contentious shrine|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} |consecration_year = 865 (Jōgan 7) |Main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alternate name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine |Festival = April 25 (Magomi Festival) |Rituals = Tsubokayu Festival (January 14)
Misogi Nagashi (July 31)
Kenkoku Festival (November 23) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |Label position = top }} '''Kawaguchi Asama Shrine''' (河口浅間神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District|ja|富士河口湖町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. It is one of the {{ill|Asama shrines|ja|浅間神社}} and holds historical rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, with its rank under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代社格制度}} being a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at {{ill|Sengen Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}}. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} has been debated, with {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)}} in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of {{ill|Fujikō|ja|富士講}} pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} * {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]]

In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} * {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]]

[[Category:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]]{{Shrine |name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kawaguchi-asama-jinja haiden and sugi (1).JPG|270px]]
"Gonishirushi" of the Seven Sacred Cedars and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|31|51.8|N|138|46|29.8|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District|ja|富士河口湖町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}} |Latitude degrees = 35 |Latitude minutes = 31 |Latitude seconds = 51.84 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 46 |Longitude seconds = 29.87 |ISO = JP-19 |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) {{ill|Contentious shrine|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} |consecration_year = 865 (Jōgan 7) |Main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alternate name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine |Festival = April 25 (Magomi Festival) |Rituals = Tsubokayu Festival (January 14)
Misogi Nagashi (July 31)
Kenkoku Festival (November 23) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |Label position = top }} '''Kawaguchi Asama Shrine''' (河口浅間神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District|ja|富士河口湖町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. It is one of the {{ill|Asama shrines|ja|浅間神社}} and holds historical rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, with its rank under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代type制度}} being a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at {{ill|Sengen Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}}. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} has been debated, with {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)}} in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of {{ill|Fujikō|ja|富士講}} pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} * {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]]

The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} has been debated, with {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)}} in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of {{ill|Fujikō|ja|富士講}} pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} * {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]]

[[Category:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]]{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kawaguchi-asama-jinja haiden and sugi (1).JPG|270px]]
"Gonishirushi" of the Seven Sacred Cedars and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|31|51.8|N|138|46|29.8|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District|ja|富士河口湖町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}} |Latitude degrees = 35 |Latitude minutes = 31 |Latitude seconds = 51.84 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 46 |Longitude seconds = 29.87 |ISO = JP-19 |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) {{ill|Contentious shrine|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} |consecration_year = 865 (Jōgan 7) |Main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alternate name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine |Festival = April 25 (Magomi Festival) |Rituals = Tsubokayu Festival (January 14)
Misogi Nagashi (July 31)
Kenkoku Festival (November 23) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |Label position = top }} '''Kawaguchi Asama Shrine''' (河口浅間神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District|ja|富士河口湖町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. It is one of the {{ill|Asama shrines|ja|浅間神社}} and holds historical rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, with its rank under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代type制度}} being a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at {{ill|Sengen Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}}. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} has been debated, with {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)}} in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of {{ill|Fujikō|ja|富士講}} pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} * {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]]

The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} * {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]]

"Gonishirushi" of the Seven Sacred Cedars and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|31|51.8|N|138|46|29.8|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District|ja|富士河口湖町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}} |Latitude degrees = 35 |Latitude minutes = 31 |Latitude seconds = 51.84 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 46 |Longitude seconds = 29.87 |ISO = JP-19 |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) {{ill|Contentious shrine|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} |consecration_year = 865 (Jōgan 7) |Main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alternate name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine |Festival = April 25 (Magomi Festival) |Rituals = Tsubokayu Festival (January 14)
Misogi Nagashi (July 31)
Kenkoku Festival (November 23) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |Label position = top }} '''Kawaguchi Asama Shrine''' (河口浅間神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District|ja|富士河口湖町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. It is one of the {{ill|Asama shrines|ja|浅間神社}} and holds historical rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, with its rank under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} being a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at {{ill|Sengen Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}}. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} has been debated, with {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)}} in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of {{ill|Fujikō|ja|富士講}} pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} * {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|31|51.8|N|138|46|29.8|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District|ja|富士河口湖町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}} |Latitude degrees = 35 |Latitude minutes = 31 |Latitude seconds = 51.84 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 46 |Longitude seconds = 29.87 |ISO = JP-19 |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) {{ill|Contentious shrine|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} |consecration_year = 865 (Jōgan 7) |Main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alternate name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine |Festival = April 25 (Magomi Festival) |Rituals = Tsubokayu Festival (January 14)
Misogi Nagashi (July 31)
Kenkoku Festival (November 23) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |Label position = top }} '''Kawaguchi Asama Shrine''' (河口浅間神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District|ja|富士河口湖町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. It is one of the {{ill|Asama shrines|ja|浅間神社}} and holds historical rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, with its rank under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} being a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at {{ill|Sengen Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}}. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} has been debated, with {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)}} in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of {{ill|Fujikō|ja|富士講}} pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} * {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]]

It is one of the {{ill|Asama shrines|ja|浅間神社}} and holds historical rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, with its rank under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} being a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at {{ill|Sengen Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}}. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} has been debated, with {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)}} in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of {{ill|Fujikō|ja|富士講}} pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} * {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]]

Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} has been debated, with {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)}} in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of {{ill|Fujikō|ja|富士講}} pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} * {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[Category:New]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine}}

According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at {{ill|Sengen Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}}. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} has been debated, with {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)}} in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of {{ill|Fujikō|ja|富士講}} pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} * {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine}}

Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} |consecration_year = 865 (Jōgan 7) |Main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alternate name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine |Festival = April 25 (Magomi Festival) |Rituals = Tsubokayu Festival (January 14)
Misogi Nagashi (July 31)
Kenkoku Festival (November 23) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |Label position = top }} '''Kawaguchi Asama Shrine''' (河口浅間神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District|ja|富士河口湖町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. It is one of the {{ill|Asama shrines|ja|浅間神社}} and holds historical rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, with its rank under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} being a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at {{ill|Sengen Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}}. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} has been debated, with {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)}} in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of {{ill|Fujikō|ja|富士講}} pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} * {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine}}[[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine}}

The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} * {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine}}

[[Category:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11 deprecated open]] [[Category:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]]{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kawaguchi-asama-jinja haiden and sugi (1).JPG|270px]]
"Gonishirushi" of the Seven Sacred Cedars and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|31|51.8|N|138|46|29.8|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District|ja|富士河口湖町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}} |Latitude degrees = 35 |Latitude minutes = 31 |Latitude seconds = 51.84 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 46 |Longitude seconds = 29.87 |ISO = JP-19 |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}) {{ill|Contentious shrine|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} |consecration_year = 865 (Jōgan 7) |Main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alternate name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine |Festival = April 25 (Magomi Festival) |Rituals = Tsubokayu Festival (January 14)
Misogi Nagashi (July 31)
Kenkoku Festival (November 23) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |Label position = top }} '''Kawaguchi Asama Shrine''' (河口浅間神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District|ja|富士河口湖町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. It is one of the {{ill|Asama shrines|ja|浅間神社}} and holds historical rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, with its rank under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} being a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at {{ill|Sengen Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}}. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} has been debated, with {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)}} in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of {{ill|Fujikō|ja|富士講}} pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} * {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine}}

Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} |consecration_year = 865 (Jōgan 7) |Main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alternate name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine |Festival = April 25 (Magomi Festival) |Rituals = Tsubokayu Festival (January 14)
Misogi Nagashi (July 31)
Kenkoku Festival (November 23) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |Label position = top }} '''Kawaguchi Asama Shrine''' (河口浅間神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District|ja|富士河口湖町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. It is one of the {{ill|Asama shrines|ja|浅間神社}} and holds historical rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, with its rank under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} being a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at {{ill|Sengen Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}}. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} has been debated, with {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)|1=Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)}} in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of {{ill|Fujikō|ja|富士講}} pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} * {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)|1=Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine}} [[Category:New]]

The shrine is a short walk north.== See Also == * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} * {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)|1=Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

"Gonishirushi" of the Seven Sacred Cedars and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|31|51.8|N|138|46|29.8|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District|ja|富士河口湖町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}} |Latitude degrees = 35 |Latitude minutes = 31 |Latitude seconds = 51.84 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 46 |Longitude seconds = 29.87 |ISO = JP-19 |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}) {{ill|Contentious shrine|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} |consecration_year = 865 (Jōgan 7) |Main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alternate name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine |Festival = April 25 (Magomi Festival) |Rituals = Tsubokayu Festival (January 14)
Misogi Nagashi (July 31)
Kenkoku Festival (November 23) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |Label position = top }} '''Kawaguchi Asama Shrine''' (河口浅間神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District|ja|富士河口湖町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. It is one of the {{ill|Asama shrines|ja|浅間神社}} and holds historical rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, with its rank under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} being a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at {{ill|Sengen Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}}. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} has been debated, with {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)|1=Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)}} in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of {{ill|Fujikō|ja|富士講}} pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also! == * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} * {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)|1=Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|31|51.8|N|138|46|29.8|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District|ja|富士河口湖町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}} |Latitude degrees = 35 |Latitude minutes = 31 |Latitude seconds = 51.84 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 46 |Longitude seconds = 29.87 |ISO = JP-19 |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}) {{ill|Contentious shrine|ja|論社}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} |consecration_year = 865 (Jōgan 7) |Main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alternate name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine |Festival = April 25 (Magomi Festival) |Rituals = Tsubokayu Festival (January 14)
Misogi Nagashi (July 31)
Kenkoku Festival (November 23) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |Label position = top }} '''Kawaguchi Asama Shrine''' (河口浅間神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District|ja|富士河口湖町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. It is one of the {{ill|Asama shrines|ja|浅間神社}} and holds historical rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, with its rank under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} being a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at {{ill|Sengen Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}}. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} has been debated, with {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)|1=Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)}} in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of {{ill|Fujikō|ja|富士講}} pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} * {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)|1=Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}} * {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)|1=Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] {{draft categories| [[Category:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[en:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|31|51.8|N|138|46|29.8|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District|ja|富士河口湖町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft}} |Latitude degrees = 35 |Latitude minutes = 31 |Latitude seconds = 51.84 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 46 |Longitude seconds = 29.87 |ISO = JP-19 |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=draft}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}}) {{ill|Contentious shrine|ja|論社|12=draft}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} |consecration_year = 865 (Jōgan 7) |Main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}}, copper roof |Alternate name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine |Festival = April 25 (Magomi Festival) |Rituals = Tsubokayu Festival (January 14)
Misogi Nagashi (July 31)
Kenkoku Festival (November 23) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |Label position = top }} '''Kawaguchi Asama Shrine''' (河口浅間神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District|ja|富士河口湖町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft}}. It is one of the {{ill|Asama shrines|ja|浅間神社|12=draft}} and holds historical rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|12=draft}}, with its rank under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} being a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=draft}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神|12=draft}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命|12=draft}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=draft}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at {{ill|Sengen Shrine|ja|浅間神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国|12=draft}}. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|12=draft}}. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} has been debated, with {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)|1=Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|12=draft}} in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of {{ill|Fujikō|ja|富士講|12=draft}} pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|12=draft}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞|12=draft}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)|1=Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] {{draft categories| [[Category:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[en:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine}}

The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)|1=Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] {{draft categories| [[Category:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] }} [[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[en:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kawaguchi-asama-jinja haiden and sugi (1).JPG|270px]]
"Gonishirushi" of the Seven Sacred Cedars and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|31|51.8|N|138|46|29.8|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District|ja|富士河口湖町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft}} |Latitude degrees = 35 |Latitude minutes = 31 |Latitude seconds = 51.84 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 46 |Longitude seconds = 29.87 |ISO = JP-19 |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=draft}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}}) {{ill|Contentious shrine|ja|論社|12=draft}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} |consecration_year = 865 (Jōgan 7) |Main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}}, copper roof |Alternate name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine |Festival = April 25 (Magomi Festival) |Rituals = Tsubokayu Festival (January 14)
Misogi Nagashi (July 31)
Kenkoku Festival (November 23) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |Label position = top }} '''Kawaguchi Asama Shrine''' (河口浅間神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District|ja|富士河口湖町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft}}. It is one of the {{ill|Asama shrines|ja|浅間神社|12=draft}} and holds historical rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|12=draft}}, with its rank under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} being a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=draft}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神|12=draft}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命|12=draft}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=draft}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at {{ill|Sengen Shrine|ja|浅間神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国|12=draft}}. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|12=draft}}. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} has been debated, with {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)|1=Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|12=draft}} in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of {{ill|Fujikō|ja|富士講|12=draft}} pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|12=draft}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞|12=draft}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)|1=Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] {{draft categories| [[Category:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[en:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine}}

"Gonishirushi" of the Seven Sacred Cedars and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|31|51.8|N|138|46|29.8|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District|ja|富士河口湖町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft}} |Latitude degrees = 35 |Latitude minutes = 31 |Latitude seconds = 51.84 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 46 |Longitude seconds = 29.87 |ISO = JP-19 |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=draft}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}}) {{ill|Contentious shrine|ja|論社|12=draft}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} |consecration_year = 865 (Jōgan 7) |Main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}}, copper roof |Alternate name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine |Festival = April 25 (Magomi Festival) |Rituals = Tsubokayu Festival (January 14)
Misogi Nagashi (July 31)
Kenkoku Festival (November 23) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |Label position = top }} '''Kawaguchi Asama Shrine''' (河口浅間神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District|ja|富士河口湖町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft}}. It is one of the {{ill|Asama shrines|ja|浅間神社|12=draft}} and holds historical rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|12=draft}}, with its rank under the {{ill|Modern type System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} being a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=draft}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神|12=draft}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命|12=draft}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=draft}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at {{ill|Sengen Shrine|ja|浅間神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国|12=draft}}. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|12=draft}}. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} has been debated, with {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)|1=Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|12=draft}} in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of {{ill|Fujikō|ja|富士講|12=draft}} pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|12=draft}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞|12=draft}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Fuefuki City Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)|1=Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[en:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine}}
