Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} |consecration_year = 865 (Jōgan 7) |Main hall = Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]], copper roof |Alternate name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine |Festival = April 25 (Magomi Festival) |Rituals = Tsubokayu Festival (January 14)
Misogi Nagashi (July 31)
Kenkoku Festival (November 23) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |Label position = top }} '''Kawaguchi Asama Shrine''' (河口浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fujikawaguchiko|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. It is one of the [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama shrines]] and holds historical Rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=}}, with its Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] being a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Ōkami|qq=}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen Shrine]] in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]]. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} has been debated, with [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of [[:en:Fujiko (religion)|Fujikō]] pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|qq=}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|qq=}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] * [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] {{translated page|ja|河口浅間神社|version=101937416|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] }} [[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[en:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Fujisan, sacred place and source of artistic inspiration]] [[Category:富士河口湖町の建築物]] [[Category:富士河口湖町の歴史]] [[Category:山梨県にある国指定の史跡]]

It is one of the [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama shrines]] and holds historical Rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=}}, with its Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] being a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Ōkami|qq=}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen Shrine]] in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]]. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} has been debated, with [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of [[:en:Fujiko (religion)|Fujikō]] pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|qq=}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|qq=}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] * [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] {{translated page|ja|河口浅間神社|version=101937416|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] }} [[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[en:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Fujisan, sacred place and source of artistic inspiration]] [[Category:富士河口湖町の建築物]] [[Category:富士河口湖町の歴史]] [[Category:山梨県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:甲斐国の式内社]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|31|51.8|N|138|46|29.8|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Fujikawaguchiko|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]] |Latitude degrees = 35 |Latitude minutes = 31 |Latitude seconds = 51.84 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 46 |Longitude seconds = 29.87 |ISO = JP-19 |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}}) {{ill|Contentious shrine|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Contentious shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} |consecration_year = 865 (Jōgan 7) |Main hall = Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]], copper roof |Alternate name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine |Festival = April 25 (Magomi Festival) |Rituals = Tsubokayu Festival (January 14)
Misogi Nagashi (July 31)
Kenkoku Festival (November 23) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |Label position = top }} '''Kawaguchi Asama Shrine''' (河口浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fujikawaguchiko|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. It is one of the [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama shrines]] and holds historical Rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=}}, with its Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] being a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Ōkami|qq=}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen Shrine]] in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]]. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} has been debated, with [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of [[:en:Fujiko (religion)|Fujikō]] pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|qq=}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|qq=}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] * [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] {{translated page|ja|河口浅間神社|version=101937416|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] }} [[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[en:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Fujisan, sacred place and source of artistic inspiration]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujikawaguchiko, Yamanashi]] [[Category:富士河口湖町の歴史]] [[Category:National historic sites in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:甲斐国の式内社]] {{Commons category|Kawaguchi Asama Shrine}}

The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} has been debated, with [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of [[:en:Fujiko (religion)|Fujikō]] pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|qq=}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] * [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] {{translated page|ja|河口浅間神社|version=101937416|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] }} [[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Fujisan, sacred place and source of artistic inspiration]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujikawaguchiko, Yamanashi]] [[Category:富士河口湖町の歴史]] [[Category:National historic sites in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:甲斐国の式内社]] {{Commons category|Kawaguchi Asama Shrine}}

"Gonishirushi" of the Seven Sacred Cedars and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|31|51.8|N|138|46|29.8|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Fujikawaguchiko|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]] |Latitude degrees = 35 |Latitude minutes = 31 |Latitude seconds = 51.84 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 46 |Longitude seconds = 29.87 |ISO = JP-19 |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|Contentious shrine|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Contentious shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}
Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |consecration_year = 865 (Jōgan 7) |Main hall = Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]], copper roof |Alternate name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine |Festival = April 25 (Magomi Festival) |Rituals = Tsubokayu Festival (January 14)
Misogi Nagashi (July 31)
Kenkoku Festival (November 23) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |Label position = top }} '''Kawaguchi Asama Shrine''' (河口浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fujikawaguchiko|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. It is one of the [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama shrines]] and holds historical Rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, with its Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] being a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 浅間神社}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen Shrine]] in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]]. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} has been debated, with [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of [[:en:Fujiko (religion)|Fujikō]] pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|qq=}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] * [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] {{translated page|ja|河口浅間神社|version=101937416|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] }} [[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Fujisan, sacred place and source of artistic inspiration]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujikawaguchiko, Yamanashi]] [[Category:富士河口湖町の歴史]] [[Category:National historic sites in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:甲斐国の式内社]] {{Commons category|Kawaguchi Asama Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kawaguchi-asama-jinja haiden and sugi (1).JPG|270px]]
"Gonishirushi" of the Seven Sacred Cedars and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|31|51.8|N|138|46|29.8|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Fujikawaguchiko|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]] |Latitude degrees = 35 |Latitude minutes = 31 |Latitude seconds = 51.84 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 46 |Longitude seconds = 29.87 |ISO = JP-19 |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|Contentious shrine|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Contentious shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}
Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |consecration_year = 865 (Jōgan 7) |Main hall = Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]], copper roof |Alternate name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine |Festival = April 25 (Magomi Festival) |Rituals = Tsubokayu Festival (January 14)
Misogi Nagashi (July 31)
Kenkoku Festival (November 23) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |Label position = top }} '''Kawaguchi Asama Shrine''' (河口浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fujikawaguchiko|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. It is one of the [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama shrines]] and holds historical Rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, with its Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] being a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 浅間神社}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen Shrine]] in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]]. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} has been debated, with [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of [[:en:Fujiko (religion)|Fujikō]] pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|qq=}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] * [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] {{translated page|ja|河口浅間神社|version=101937416|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] }} [[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Fujisan, sacred place and source of artistic inspiration]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujikawaguchiko, Yamanashi]] [[Category:富士河口湖町の歴史]] [[Category:National historic sites in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kawaguchi Asama Shrine}}

It is one of the [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama shrines]] and holds historical Rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, with its Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] being a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 浅間神社}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen Shrine]] in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]]. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} has been debated, with [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of [[:en:Fujiko (religion)|Fujikō]] pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|qq=}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] * [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] {{translated page|ja|河口浅間神社|version=101937416|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] }} [[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Fujisan, sacred place and source of artistic inspiration]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujikawaguchiko, Yamanashi]] [[Category:富士河口湖町の歴史]] [[Category:National historic sites in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kawaguchi Asama Shrine}}

It is one of the [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama shrines]] and holds historical Rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, with its Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] being a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 浅間神社}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen Shrine]] in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]]. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} has been debated, with [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of [[:en:Fujiko (religion)|Fujikō]] pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|qq=}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] * [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] {{translated page|ja|河口浅間神社|version=101937416|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] }} [[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Fujisan, sacred place and source of artistic inspiration]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujikawaguchiko, Yamanashi]] [[Category:富士河口湖町の歴史]] [[Category:National historic sites in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kawaguchi Asama Shrine}}

Misogi Nagashi (July 31)
Kenkoku Festival (November 23) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |Label position = top }} '''Kawaguchi Asama Shrine''' (河口浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fujikawaguchiko|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. It is one of the [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama shrines]] and holds historical Rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, with its Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] being a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 浅間神社}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen Shrine]] in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]]. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} has been debated, with [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of [[:en:Fujiko (religion)|Fujikō]] pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|qq=}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] * [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] {{translated page|ja|河口浅間神社|version=101937416|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] }} [[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Fujisan, sacred place and source of artistic inspiration]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujikawaguchiko, Yamanashi]] [[Category:富士河口湖の Municipal History]] [[Category:National historic sites in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kawaguchi Asama Shrine}}

Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} has been debated, with [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of [[:en:Fujiko (religion)|Fujikō]] pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|qq=}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] * [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] {{translated page|ja|河口浅間神社|version=101937416|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] }} [[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Fujisan, sacred place and source of artistic inspiration]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujikawaguchiko, Yamanashi]] [[Category:富士河口湖の Municipal History]] [[Category:National historic sites in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kawaguchi Asama Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kawaguchi-asama-jinja haiden and sugi (1).JPG|270px]]
"Gonishirushi" of the Seven Sacred Cedars and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|31|51.8|N|138|46|29.8|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Fujikawaguchiko|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]] |Latitude degrees = 35 |Latitude minutes = 31 |Latitude seconds = 51.84 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 46 |Longitude seconds = 29.87 |ISO = JP-19 |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|Contentious shrine|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Contentious shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}
Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |consecration_year = 865 (Jōgan 7) |Main hall = Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]], copper roof |Alternate name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine |Festival = April 25 (Magomi Festival) |Rituals = Tsubokayu Festival (January 14)
Misogi Nagashi (July 31)
Kenkoku Festival (November 23) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |Label position = top }} '''Kawaguchi Asama Shrine''' (河口浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fujikawaguchiko|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. It is one of the [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama shrines]] and holds historical Rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}, with its Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] being a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 浅間神社}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen Shrine]] in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]]. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} has been debated, with [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of [[:en:Fujiko (religion)|Fujikō]] pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|qq=}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] * [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] {{translated page|ja|河口浅間神社|version=101937416|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] }} [[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Fujisan, sacred place and source of artistic inspiration]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujikawaguchiko, Yamanashi]] [[Category:富士河口湖の Municipal History]] [[Category:National historic sites in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kawaguchi Asama Shrine}}

Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} has been debated, with [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of [[:en:Fujiko (religion)|Fujikō]] pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|qq=}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] * [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] {{translated page|ja|河口浅間神社|version=101937416|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] }} [[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Fujisan, sacred place and source of artistic inspiration]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujikawaguchiko, Yamanashi]] [[Category:History of Fujikawaguchiko Town]] [[Category:National historic sites in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kawaguchi Asama Shrine}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11553669]] Wikidata name: Kawaguchi Asama Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kawaguchi-asama-jinja haiden and sugi (1).JPG|270px]]
"Gonishirushi" of the Seven Sacred Cedars and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|31|51.8|N|138|46|29.8|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Fujikawaguchiko|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]] |Latitude degrees = 35 |Latitude minutes = 31 |Latitude seconds = 51.84 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 46 |Longitude seconds = 29.87 |ISO = JP-19 |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|Contentious shrine|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Contentious shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}
Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |consecration_year = 865 (Jōgan 7) |Main hall = Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]], copper roof |Alternate name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine |Festival = April 25 (Magomi Festival) |Rituals = Tsubokayu Festival (January 14)
Misogi Nagashi (July 31)
Kenkoku Festival (November 23) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |Label position = top }} '''Kawaguchi Asama Shrine''' (河口浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fujikawaguchiko|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. It is one of the [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama shrines]] and holds historical Rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}, with its Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] being a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 浅間神社}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen Shrine]] in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]]. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} has been debated, with [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of [[:en:Fujiko (religion)|Fujikō]] pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|qq=}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] * [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] {{translated page|ja|河口浅間神社|version=101937416|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] }} [[sn:Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine]] [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Fujisan, sacred place and source of artistic inspiration]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujikawaguchiko, Yamanashi]] [[Category:History of Fujikawaguchiko Town]] [[Category:National historic sites in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kawaguchi Asama Shrine}}
