"Gonishirushi" of the Seven Sacred Cedars and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|31|51.8|N|138|46|29.8|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Fujikawaguchiko|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]] |Latitude degrees = 35 |Latitude minutes = 31 |Latitude seconds = 51.84 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 46 |Longitude seconds = 29.87 |ISO = JP-19 |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|Contentious shrine|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Contentious shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}

Misogi Nagashi (July 31)
Kenkoku Festival (November 23) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |Label position = top }} '''Kawaguchi Asama Shrine''' (河口浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fujikawaguchiko|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. It is one of the [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama shrines]] and holds historical Rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}, with its Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] being a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 浅間神社|1=Sengen shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3471785}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen Shrine]] in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]]. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} has been debated, with [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of [[:en:Fujiko (religion)|Fujikō]] pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|1=Kawaguchi no Chigomai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553660}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] * [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] {{translated page|ja|河口浅間神社|version=101937416|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] }} [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Fujisan, sacred place and source of artistic inspiration]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujikawaguchiko, Yamanashi]] [[Category:History of Fujikawaguchiko Town]] [[Category:National historic sites in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kai Province]] {{Commons category|Kawaguchi Asama Shrine}} {{wikidata link|Q11553669}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

"Gonishirushi" of the Seven Sacred Cedars and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|31|51.8|N|138|46|29.8|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Fujikawaguchiko|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]] |Latitude degrees = 35 |Latitude minutes = 31 |Latitude seconds = 51.84 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 46 |Longitude seconds = 29.87 |ISO = JP-19 |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|Contentious shrine|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Contentious shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}
Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = 865 (Jōgan 7) |Main hall = Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]], copper roof |Alternate name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine |Festival = April 25 (Magomi Festival) |Rituals = Tsubokayu Festival (January 14)
Misogi Nagashi (July 31)
Kenkoku Festival (November 23) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |Label position = top }} '''Kawaguchi Asama Shrine''' (河口浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fujikawaguchiko|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. It is one of the [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama shrines]] and holds historical Rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}, with its Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] being a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 浅間神社|1=Sengen shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3471785}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen Shrine]] in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]]. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} has been debated, with [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of [[:en:Fujiko (religion)|Fujikō]] pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|1=Kawaguchi no Chigomai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553660}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] * [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] {{translated page|ja|河口浅間神社|version=101937416|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] }} [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Fujisan, sacred place and source of artistic inspiration]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujikawaguchiko, Yamanashi]] [[Category:History of Fujikawaguchiko Town]] [[Category:National historic sites in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kai Province]] {{Commons category|Kawaguchi Asama Shrine}} {{wikidata link|Q11553669}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

"Gonishirushi" of the Seven Sacred Cedars and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|31|51.8|N|138|46|29.8|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Fujikawaguchiko|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]] |Latitude degrees = 35 |Latitude minutes = 31 |Latitude seconds = 51.84 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 46 |Longitude seconds = 29.87 |ISO = JP-19 |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|Contentious shrine|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Contentious shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}
Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = 865 (Jōgan 7) |Main hall = Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]], copper roof |Alternate name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine |Festival = April 25 (Magomi Festival) |Rituals = Tsubokayu Festival (January 14)
Misogi Nagashi (July 31)
Kenkoku Festival (November 23) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |Label position = top }} '''Kawaguchi Asama Shrine''' (河口浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fujikawaguchiko|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. It is one of the [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama shrines]] and holds historical Rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}, with its Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] being a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 浅間神社|1=Sengen shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3471785}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen Shrine]] in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]]. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} has been debated, with [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of [[:en:Fujiko (religion)|Fujikō]] pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|1=Kawaguchi no Chigomai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553660}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] * [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] {{translated page|ja|河口浅間神社|version=101937416|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] }} [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Fujisan, sacred place and source of artistic inspiration]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujikawaguchiko, Yamanashi]] [[Category:History of Fujikawaguchiko Town]] [[Category:National historic sites in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kai Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{Commons category|Kawaguchi Asama Shrine}} {{wikidata link|Q11553669}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

It is one of the [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama shrines]] and holds historical Rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}, with its Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] being a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = 865 (Jōgan 7) |Main hall = Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]], copper roof |Alternate name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine |Festival = April 25 (Magomi Festival) |Rituals = Tsubokayu Festival (January 14)
Misogi Nagashi (July 31)
Kenkoku Festival (November 23) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |Label position = top }} '''Kawaguchi Asama Shrine''' (河口浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fujikawaguchiko|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. It is one of the [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama shrines]] and holds historical Rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}, with its Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] being a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 浅間神社|1=Sengen shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3471785}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen Shrine]] in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]]. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} has been debated, with [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of [[:en:Fujiko (religion)|Fujikō]] pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|1=Kawaguchi no Chigomai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553660}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] * [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] {{translated page|ja|河口浅間神社|version=101937416|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] }} [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Fujisan, sacred place and source of artistic inspiration]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujikawaguchiko, Yamanashi]] [[Category:History of Fujikawaguchiko Town]] [[Category:National historic sites in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kai Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{Commons category|Kawaguchi Asama Shrine}} {{wikidata link|Q11553669}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine (Q11553669)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Kai Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine|lt=Kawaguchi Asama Shrine|ja|河口浅間神社|commons|Category:Kawaguchi Asama Shrine|WD=Q11553669}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei}} || — || part of {{ill|Fujisan, sacred place and source of artistic inspiration|WD=Q16482294}}, {{ill|ichinomiya|WD=Q1656379|lt=ichinomiya}} || {{ill|Asamano Shrine|lt=Asamano Shrine|WD=Q135039708}} || {{coord|35.531056|138.774944|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181673 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11553669}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kai Province]] [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|31|51.8|N|138|46|29.8|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Fujikawaguchiko|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]] |Latitude degrees = 35 |Latitude minutes = 31 |Latitude seconds = 51.84 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 46 |Longitude seconds = 29.87 |ISO = JP-19 |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|Contentious shrine|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Contentious shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}
Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = 865 (Jōgan 7) |Main hall = Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]], copper roof |Alternate name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine |Festival = April 25 (Magomi Festival) |Rituals = Tsubokayu Festival (January 14)
Misogi Nagashi (July 31)
Kenkoku Festival (November 23) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |Label position = top }} '''Kawaguchi Asama Shrine''' (河口浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fujikawaguchiko|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. It is one of the [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama shrines]] and holds historical Rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}, with its Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] being a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 浅間神社|1=Sengen shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3471785}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen Shrine]] in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]]. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} has been debated, with [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of [[:en:Fujiko (religion)|Fujikō]] pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|1=Kawaguchi no Chigomai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553660}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] * [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|河口浅間神社|version=101937416|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] }} [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Fujisan, sacred place and source of artistic inspiration]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujikawaguchiko, Yamanashi]] [[Category:History of Fujikawaguchiko Town]] [[Category:National historic sites in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kai Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{Commons category|Kawaguchi Asama Shrine}} {{wikidata link|Q11553669}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine (Q11553669)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Kai Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine|lt=Kawaguchi Asama Shrine|ja|河口浅間神社|commons|Category:Kawaguchi Asama Shrine|WD=Q11553669}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || — || part of {{ill|Fujisan, sacred place and source of artistic inspiration|WD=Q16482294}}, {{ill|ichinomiya|WD=Q1656379|lt=ichinomiya}} || {{ill|Asamano Shrine|lt=Asamano Shrine|WD=Q135039708}} || {{coord|35.531056|138.774944|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181673 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11553669}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kai Province]] [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Kawaguchi Asama Shrine]][[ja:河口浅間神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11553669]] Wikidata name: Kawaguchi Asama Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11553669]] Wikidata name: Kawaguchi Asama Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kawaguchi-asama-jinja haiden and sugi (1).JPG|270px]]
"Gonishirushi" of the Seven Sacred Cedars and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|31|51.8|N|138|46|29.8|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Fujikawaguchiko|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]] |Latitude degrees = 35 |Latitude minutes = 31 |Latitude seconds = 51.84 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 46 |Longitude seconds = 29.87 |ISO = JP-19 |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|Contentious shrine|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Contentious shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}
Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = 865 (Jōgan 7) |Main hall = Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]], copper roof |Alternate name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine |Festival = April 25 (Magomi Festival) |Rituals = Tsubokayu Festival (January 14)
Misogi Nagashi (July 31)
Kenkoku Festival (November 23) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |Label position = top }} '''Kawaguchi Asama Shrine''' (河口浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fujikawaguchiko|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. It is one of the [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama shrines]] and holds historical Rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}, with its Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] being a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 浅間神社|1=Sengen shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3471785}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen Shrine]] in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]]. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} has been debated, with [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of [[:en:Fujiko (religion)|Fujikō]] pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|1=Kawaguchi no Chigomai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553660}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] * [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|河口浅間神社|version=101937416|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] }} [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Fujisan, sacred place and source of artistic inspiration]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujikawaguchiko, Yamanashi]] [[Category:History of Fujikawaguchiko Town]] [[Category:National historic sites in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kai Province]] {{Commons category|Kawaguchi Asama Shrine}} {{wikidata link|Q11553669}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine (Q11553669)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Kai Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine|lt=Kawaguchi Asama Shrine|ja|河口浅間神社|commons|Category:Kawaguchi Asama Shrine|WD=Q11553669}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || — || part of {{ill|Fujisan, sacred place and source of artistic inspiration|WD=Q16482294}}, {{ill|ichinomiya|WD=Q1656379|lt=ichinomiya}} || {{ill|Asamano Shrine|lt=Asamano Shrine|WD=Q135039708}} || {{coord|35.531056|138.774944|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181673 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11553669}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kai Province]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]

Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} has been debated, with [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of [[:en:Fujiko (religion)|Fujikō]] pilgrimages in the Edo period. The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] * [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|河口浅間神社|version=101937416|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] }} [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Fujisan, sacred place and source of artistic inspiration]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujikawaguchiko, Yamanashi]] [[Category:History of Fujikawaguchiko Town]] [[Category:National historic sites in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kai Province]] {{Commons category|Kawaguchi Asama Shrine}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine (Q11553669)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. [[sn:commons:Category:Kawaguchi Asama Shrine]][[ja:河口浅間神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11553669]] Wikidata name: Kawaguchi Asama Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11553669]] Wikidata name: Kawaguchi Asama Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kawaguchi-asama-jinja haiden and sugi (1).JPG|270px]]
"Gonishirushi" of the Seven Sacred Cedars and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|31|51.8|N|138|46|29.8|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Fujikawaguchiko|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]] |Latitude degrees = 35 |Latitude minutes = 31 |Latitude seconds = 51.84 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 46 |Longitude seconds = 29.87 |ISO = JP-19 |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|Contentious shrine|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Contentious shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}
Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = 865 (Jōgan 7) |Main hall = Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]], copper roof |Alternate name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine |Festival = April 25 (Magomi Festival) |Rituals = Tsubokayu Festival (January 14)
Misogi Nagashi (July 31)
Kenkoku Festival (November 23) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |Label position = top }} '''Kawaguchi Asama Shrine''' (河口浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fujikawaguchiko|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. It is one of the [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama shrines]] and holds historical Rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}, with its Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] being a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 浅間神社|1=Sengen shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3471785}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen Shrine]] in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]]. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} has been debated, with [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of [[:en:Fujiko (religion)|Fujikō]] pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|1=Kawaguchi no Chigomai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553660}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] * [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|河口浅間神社|version=101937416|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] }} [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Fujisan, sacred place and source of artistic inspiration]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujikawaguchiko, Yamanashi]] [[Category:History of Fujikawaguchiko Town]] [[Category:National historic sites in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kai Province]] {{Commons category|Kawaguchi Asama Shrine}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine (Q11553669)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kai Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine|lt=Kawaguchi Asama Shrine|ja|河口浅間神社|commons|Category:Kawaguchi Asama Shrine|WD=Q11553669}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || — || part of {{ill|Fujisan, sacred place and source of artistic inspiration|WD=Q16482294}}, {{ill|ichinomiya|WD=Q1656379|lt=ichinomiya}} || {{ill|Asamano Shrine|lt=Asamano Shrine|WD=Q135039708}} || {{coord|35.531056|138.774944|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181673 DB] |} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kai Province]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] {{wikidata link|Q11553669}}

[[commons:Category:Kawaguchi Asama Shrine]][[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[commons:Category:Kawaguchi Asama Shrine]][[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kawaguchi Asama Shrine]][[ja:河口浅間神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11553669]] Wikidata name: Kawaguchi Asama Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11553669]] Wikidata name: Kawaguchi Asama Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kawaguchi-asama-jinja haiden and sugi (1).JPG|270px]]
"Gonishirushi" of the Seven Sacred Cedars and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|31|51.8|N|138|46|29.8|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Fujikawaguchiko|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]] |Latitude degrees = 35 |Latitude minutes = 31 |Latitude seconds = 51.84 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 46 |Longitude seconds = 29.87 |ISO = JP-19 |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|Contentious shrine|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Contentious shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}
Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = 865 (Jōgan 7) |Main hall = Single-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]], copper roof |Alternate name = Kawaguchi Asama Shrine |Festival = April 25 (Magomi Festival) |Rituals = Tsubokayu Festival (January 14)
Misogi Nagashi (July 31)
Kenkoku Festival (November 23) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |Label position = top }} '''Kawaguchi Asama Shrine''' (河口浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fujikawaguchiko|Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. It is one of the [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama shrines]] and holds historical Rank as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}, with its Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] being a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is part of the World Cultural Heritage designation as a component of "Mount Fuji - Object of Worship and Source of Artistic Inspiration." == deity == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, referred to as {{ill|Asama Ōkami|ja|浅間大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 浅間神社|1=Sengen shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3471785}} by the shrine. According to the shrine's records, her husband, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, and her father, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, are also worshipped in the same hall. == History == === Foundation === The shrine was established in 865 (Jōgan 7) to quell the eruption of Mount Fuji that began in 864. The eruption caused extensive damage, which was believed to result from neglect of worship at [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen Shrine]] in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]]. Consequently, it was decided to enshrine Asama Ōkami in [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The shrine's role as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} has been debated, with [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] in Fuefuki City being another possible site. === Development === The surrounding area developed as a hub for Mount Fuji worship but declined with the rise of [[:en:Fujiko (religion)|Fujikō]] pilgrimages in the Edo period. In 1871 (Meiji 4), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, later promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1924 (Taishō 13). == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Part of the "Mount Fuji" Historic Site designation. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * {{ill|Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|ja|河口の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo no Mai of Kawaguchi|1=Kawaguchi no Chigomai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553660}}, designated in 2017. === Yamanashi Prefectural Natural Monuments === * The "Seven Sacred Cedars" at the shrine are over 1,200 years old. == Access == * Bus: From Kawaguchiko Station (Fujikyuko Line), take a bus bound for Kōfu or Ōishi and get off at "Kawaguchi Post Office." The shrine is a short walk north. == See Also == * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]] * [[:en:Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|Fuefuki City Asama Shrine]] == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7137 Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|河口浅間神社|version=101937416|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] }} [[ja:河口浅間神社]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Fujisan, sacred place and source of artistic inspiration]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujikawaguchiko, Yamanashi]] [[Category:History of Fujikawaguchiko Town]] [[Category:National historic sites in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kai Province]] {{Commons category|Kawaguchi Asama Shrine}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine (Q11553669)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kai Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine|lt=Kawaguchi Asama Shrine|ja|河口浅間神社|commons|Category:Kawaguchi Asama Shrine|WD=Q11553669}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || — || part of {{ill|Fujisan, sacred place and source of artistic inspiration|WD=Q16482294}}, {{ill|ichinomiya|WD=Q1656379|lt=ichinomiya}} || {{ill|Asamano Shrine|lt=Asamano Shrine|WD=Q135039708}} || {{coord|35.531056|138.774944|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181673 DB] |} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kai Province]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] {{wikidata link|Q11553669}}
